name: NetworkTickrateHandshake
briefDescription: Internal class to manage the tickrate handshake.
description: Whenever a new peer joins, they exchange their configured tickrate with the host. If the tickrate mismatches, a warning is emitted by default, as this is assumed to be a developer mistake. [br][br]However, if this is expected, different actions can be configured.

methods.name: run
methods.description: Run the tickrate handshake. [br][br]This will connect to signals, so that every new peer receives tickrate info from the host. [br][br]Called by [_NetworkTime], no need to call manually.

methods.name: stop
methods.description: Stop the tickrate handshake. [br][br]Called by [_NetworkTime], no need to call manually.

members.name: mismatch_action
members.description: Configures what happens on a tickrate mismatch.[br]Defaults to [constant WARN], based on project settings.

constants.name: WARN
constants.description: Emit a warning on tickrate mismatch

constants.name: DISCONNECT
constants.description: Disconnect peer on tickrate mismatch[br]This is enforced by the host.

constants.name: ADJUST
constants.description: Adjust tickrate to the host's on mismatch

constants.name: SIGNAL
constants.description: Emit [signal on_tickrate_mismatch] on mismatch[br]This is emitted on both host and client.

signals.name: on_tickrate_mismatch
signals.description: Emitted when a tickrate mismatch is encountered, and [member mismatch_action] is set to [constant SIGNAL].

name: NetworkWeapon
briefDescription: Base class for creating responsive weapons, by spawning projectiles locally, but keeping control on the server.

methods.name: can_fire
methods.description: Check whether this weapon can be fired.

methods.name: fire
methods.description: Try to fire the weapon and return the projectile. [br][br]Returns null if the weapon can't be fired.

methods.name: get_fired_tick
_can_fire
methods.description: Override this method with your own can fire logic. [br][br]This can be used to implement e.g. firing cooldowns and ammo checks.

methods.name: _can_peer_use
methods.description: Override this method to check if a given peer can use this weapon. [br][br]Usually this should check if the weapon's owner is trying to fire it, but for some special cases this can be some different logic, e.g. weapons that can be used by any player on a given team.

methods.name: _after_fire
methods.description: Override this method to run any logic needed after successfully firing the weapon. [br][br]This can be used to e.g. reset the firing cooldown or deduct ammo.

methods.name: _spawn
methods.description: Override this method to spawn and initialize a projectile. [br][br]Make sure to return the projectile spawned!

methods.name: _get_data
methods.description: Override this method to extract projectile data that should be synchronized over the network. [br][br]This will be captured both locally and on the server, and will be used for reconciliation.

methods.name: _apply_data
methods.description: Override this method to apply projectile data that should be synchronized over the network. [br][br]This is used in cases where some other client fires a weapon and the server instructs us to spawn a projectile for it.

methods.name: _is_reconcilable
methods.description: Override this method to check if two projectile states can be reconciled. [br][br]This can be used to prevent cheating, for example by not allowing the client to say it's firing from the other side of the map compared to its actual position. [br][br]When this method returns false, the server will decline the projectile request.

methods.name: _reconcile
methods.description: Override this method to reconcile the initial local and remote projectile state. [br][br]Let's say the projectile travels in a straight line from its origin, but we receive a different origin from the server. In this reconciliation step, the projectile's position can be adjusted to account for the different origin. [br][br]Unless the use case is niche, the best practice is to consider the server's state as authorative.

methods.name: _can_fire
methods.description: See [NetworkWeapon]

methods.name: _can_peer_use
methods.description: See [NetworkWeapon]

methods.name: _after_fire
methods.description: See [NetworkWeapon]

methods.name: _spawn
methods.description: See [NetworkWeapon]

members.name: distance_threshold
members.description: Distance to consider too large during reconciliation checks.

name: NetworkWeaponHitscan3D
briefDescription: A 3D-specific implementation of a networked hitscan (raycast) weapon.

methods.name: fire
methods.description: Try to fire the weapon and return the projectile. [br][br]Returns true if the weapon was fired.

methods.name: can_fire
methods.description: Check whether this weapon can be fired.

methods.name: _can_fire
methods.description: Override this method with your own can fire logic. [br][br]See [NetworkWeapon].

methods.name: _can_peer_use
methods.description: Override this method to check if a given peer can use this weapon. [br][br]See [NetworkWeapon].

methods.name: _after_fire
methods.description: Override this method to run any logic needed after successfully firing the weapon. [br][br]See [NetworkWeapon].

methods.name: _on_hit
methods.description: Override to implement raycast hit logic. [br][br]The parameter is the result of a [method PhysicsDirectSpaceState3D.intersect_ray] call.

methods.name: _on_fire
methods.description: Override to implement firing effects, like muzzle flash or sound.

members.name: max_distance
members.description: Maximum distance to cast the ray

members.name: collision_mask
members.description: Mask used to detect raycast hits

members.name: exclude
members.description: Colliders excluded from raycast hits

methods.name: is_enabled
methods.description: Check if performance monitoring is enabled. [br][br]By default, monitoring is only enabled in debug builds ( see [method OS.is_debug_build] ). [br]Can be forced on with the [code]netfox_perf[/code] feature tag. [br]Can be forced off with the [code]netfox_noperf[/code] feature tag.

methods.name: get_network_loop_duration_ms
methods.description: Get time spent in the last network tick loop, in millisec. [br]Note that this also includes time spent in the rollback tick loop.

methods.name: get_network_ticks
methods.description: Get the number of ticks simulated in the last network tick loop.

methods.name: get_rollback_loop_duration_ms
methods.description: Get time spent in the last rollback tick loop, in millisec.

methods.name: get_rollback_ticks
methods.description: Get the number of ticks resimulated in the last rollback tick loop.

methods.name: get_rollback_tick_duration_ms
methods.description: Get the average amount of time spent in a rollback tick during the last rollback loop, in millisec.

methods.name: get_rollback_nodes_simulated
methods.description: Get the number of nodes simulated during the last rollback loop.

methods.name: get_rollback_nodes_simulated_per_tick
methods.description: Get the number of nodes simulated per tick on average during the last rollback loop.

methods.name: get_full_state_props_count
methods.description: Get the number of properties in the full state recorded during the last tick loop.

methods.name: get_sent_state_props_count
methods.description: Get the number of properties actually sent during the last tick loop.

methods.name: get_sent_state_props_ratio
methods.description: Get the ratio of sent properties count to full state properties count.  See [member get_full_state_props_count][br]See [member get_sent_state_props_count]

name: _NetworkSynchronizationServer
description: Handles synchronization of rollback and state properties ( [RollbackSynchronizer] and [StateSynchronizer] ), while respecting visibility filters and schemas for serialization. [br][br]Packets are sent per tick, instead of per object. So for every simulated rollback tick, a packet is sent with states, and for every recorded input, a packet is sent with the inputs. [br][br]Optionally, diff states can be used, sending only the property values that have changed, saving on bandwidth.

methods.name: register_rollback_state
methods.description: Register a [param]property[/param] of [param]node[/param] to be synchronized as rollback state

methods.name: deregister_rollback_state
methods.description: Deregister a [param]property[/param] of [param]node[/param] from being synchronized as rollback state

methods.name: register_rollback_input
methods.description: Register a [param]property[/param] of [param]node[/param] to be synchronized as rollback input

methods.name: deregister_rollback_input
methods.description: Deregister a [param]property[/param] of [param]node[/param] from being synchronized as rollback input

methods.name: register_sync_state
methods.description: Register a [param]property[/param] of [param]node[/param] to be synchronized as synchronized state

methods.name: deregister_sync_state
methods.description: Deregister a [param]property[/param] of [param]node[/param] from being synchronized as synchronized state

methods.name: register_schema
methods.description: Register a [param]serializer[/param] to use when transmitting [param]property[/param] of [param]node[/param] over the network

methods.name: deregister_schema
methods.description: Deregister any serializers used for [param]property[/param] on [param]node[/param] when transmitting over the network

methods.name: deregister_schema_for
methods.description: Deregister all serializers registered for any properties of [param]node[/param]

methods.name: register_visibility_filter
methods.description: Register a visibility [param]filter[/param] for use with [param]node[/param]

methods.name: deregister_visibility_filter
methods.description: Deregister the visibility filter used for [param]node[/param]

methods.name: deregister
methods.description: Deregister any and all settings associated with [param]node[/param]

methods.name: start
methods.description: Start NetworkTime.  Once this is called, time will be synchronized and ticks will be consistently emitted. [br][br]On clients, the initial time sync must complete before any ticks are emitted. [br][br]To check if this initial sync is done, see [method is_initial_sync_done]. If you need a signal, see [signal after_sync]. [br][br]Returns [constant OK] on success.[br]Returns [constant ERR_ALREADY_IN_USE] if the tick loop is currently active.[br]Returns [constant ERR_UNAVAILABLE] if there's no available [MultiplayerPeer].

methods.name: stop
methods.description: Stop NetworkTime.  This will stop the time sync in the background, and no more ticks will be emitted until the next start.

methods.name: is_initial_sync_done
methods.description: Check if the initial time sync is done.

methods.name: is_client_synced
methods.description: Check if client's time sync is complete.  Using this from a client is considered an error.

methods.name: ticks_to_seconds
methods.description: Convert a duration of ticks to seconds.

methods.name: seconds_to_ticks
methods.description: Convert a duration of seconds to ticks.

methods.name: seconds_between
methods.description: Calculate the duration between two ticks in seconds  [i]Note:[/i] Returns negative values if tick_to is smaller than tick_from

methods.name: ticks_between
methods.description: Calculate the duration between two points in time as ticks  [i]Note:[/i] Returns negative values if seconds_to is smaller than seconds_from

members.name: tickrate
members.description: Number of ticks per second.  [i]read-only[/i], you can change this in the project settings

members.name: sync_to_physics
members.description: Whether to sync the network ticks to physics updates.  When set to true, tickrate will be the same as the physics ticks per second, and the network tick loop will be run inside the physics update process.  [i]read-only[/i], you can change this in the project settings

members.name: max_ticks_per_frame
members.description: Maximum number of ticks to simulate per frame.  If the game itself runs slower than the configured tickrate, multiple ticks will be run in a single go. However, to avoid an endless feedback loop of running too many ticks in a frame, which makes the game even slower, which results in even more ticks and so on, this setting is an upper limit on how many ticks can be simulated in a single go.  [i]read-only[/i], you can change this in the project settings

members.name: time
members.description: Current network time in seconds.  Time is measured from the start of NetworkTime, in practice this is often the time from the server's start.  Use this value in cases where timestamps need to be shared with the server.  [i]Note:[/i] Time is continuously synced with the server. If the difference between local and server time is above a certain threshold, this value will be adjusted.  See [NetworkTimeSynchronizer]. See the setting [code]"netfox/time/recalibrate_threshold"[/code].  [i]read-only[/i]

members.name: tick
members.description: Current network time in ticks.  Time is measured from the start of NetworkTime, in practice this is often the time from the server's start.  Use this value in cases where timestamps need to be shared with the server.  [i]Note:[/i] Time is continuously synced with the server. If the difference between local and server time is above a certain threshold, this value will be adjusted.  See [NetworkTimeSynchronizer]. See the setting [code]"netfox/time/recalibrate_threshold"[/code].  [i]read-only[/i]

members.name: recalibrate_threshold
members.description: Threshold before recalibrating [member tick] and [member time].  Time is continuously synced to the server. In case the time difference is excessive between local and the server, both [code]tick[/code] and [code]time[/code] will be reset to the estimated server values. [br][br]This property determines the difference threshold in seconds for recalibration. [br][br][i]read-only[/i], you can change this in the project settings [br][br]

members.name: stall_threshold
members.description: Seconds required to pass before considering the game stalled.  If the game becomes unresponsive for some time - e.g. it becomes minimized, unfocused, or freezes -, the game time needs to be readjusted. These stalls are detected by checking how much time passes between frames. If it's more than this threshold, it's considered a stall, and will be compensated against.

members.name: remote_tick
members.description: Current network time in ticks on the server.  This is value is only an estimate, and is regularly updated. This means that this value can and probably will change depending on network conditions. [br][br][i]read-only[/i] [br][br]

members.name: remote_time
members.description: Current network time in seconds on the server.  This is value is only an estimate, and is regularly updated. This means that this value can and probably will change depending on network conditions. [br][br][i]read-only[/i] [br][br]

members.name: remote_rtt
members.description: Estimated roundtrip time to server.  This value is updated regularly, during server time sync. Latency can be estimated as half of the roundtrip time. Returns the same as [member _NetworkTimeSynchronizer.rtt]. [br][br]Will always be 0 on servers. [br][br][i]read-only[/i]

members.name: local_tick
members.description: Current network time in ticks.  On clients, this value is synced to the server [i]only once[/i] when joining the game. After that, it will increase monotonically, incrementing every single tick. [br][br]When hosting, this value is simply the number of ticks since game start. [br][br]This property can be used for things that require a timer that is guaranteed to be linear, i.e. no jumps in time. [br][br][i]read-only[/i] [br][br]

members.name: local_time
members.description: Current network time in seconds.  On clients, this value is synced to the server [i]only once[/i] when joining the game. After that, it will increase monotonically, incrementing every single tick. [br][br]When hosting, this value is simply the seconds elapsed since game start. [br][br]This property can be used for things that require a timer that is guaranteed to be linear, i.e. no jumps in time. [br][br][i]read-only[/i] [br][br]

members.name: ticktime
members.description: Amount of time a single tick takes, in seconds.  This is the reverse of tickrate  [i]read-only[/i]

members.name: tick_factor
members.description: Percentage of where we are in time for the current tick.  0.0 - the current tick just happened[br]0.5 - we're halfway to the next tick[br]1.0 - the next tick is right about to be simulated[br][i]read-only[/i]

members.name: physics_factor
members.description: Multiplier to get from physics process speeds to tick speeds.  Some methods, like CharacterBody's move_and_slide take velocity in units/sec and figure out the time delta on their own. However, they are not aware of netfox's time, so motion is all wrong in a network tick. For example, the network ticks run at 30 fps, while the game is running at 60fps, thus move_and_slide will also assume that it's running on 60fps, resulting in slower than expected movement.  To circument this, you can multiply any velocities with this variable, and get the desired speed. Don't forget to then divide by this value if it's a persistent variable ( e.g. CharacterBody's velocity ).  NOTE: This works correctly both in regular and in physics frames, but may yield different values.  [i]read-only[/i]

members.name: clock_stretch_max
members.description: The maximum clock stretch factor allowed.  For more context on clock stretch, see [member clock_stretch_factor]. The minimum allowed clock stretch factor is derived as 1.0 / clock_stretch_max. Setting this to larger values will allow for quicker clock adjustment at the cost of bigger deviations in game speed. [br][br]Make sure to adjust this value based on the game's needs. [br][br][i]read-only[/i], you can change this in the project settings

members.name: suppress_offline_peer_warning
members.description: Suppress warning when calling [member start] with an [OfflineMultiplayerPeer] active.

members.name: clock_stretch_factor
members.description: The currently used clock stretch factor.  As the game progresses, the simulation clock may be ahead of, or behind the host's remote clock. To compensate, whenever the simulation clock is ahead of the remote clock, the game will slightly slow down, to allow the remote clock to catch up. When the remote clock is ahead of the simulation clock, the game will run slightly faster to catch up with the remote clock. [br][br]This value indicates the current clock speed multiplier. Values over 1.0 indicate speeding up, under 1.0 indicate slowing down. [br][br]See [member clock_stretch_max] for clock stretch bounds.[br]See [_NetworkTimeSynchronizer] for more on the reference- and simulation clock. [br][br][i]read-only[/i]

members.name: clock_offset
members.description: The current estimated offset between the reference clock and the simulation clock.  Positive values mean the simulation clock is behind, and needs to run slightly faster to catch up. Negative values mean the simulation clock is ahead, and needs to slow down slightly. [br][br]See [member clock_stretch] for more clock speed adjustment. [br][br][i]read-only[/i]

members.name: remote_clock_offset
members.description: The current estimated offset between the reference clock and the remote clock.  Positive values mean the reference clock is behind the remote clock. Negative values mean the reference clock is ahead of the remote clock. [br][br]Returns the same as [member _NetworkTimeSynchronizer.remote_offset]. [br][br][i]read-only[/i]

signals.name: before_tick_loop
signals.description: Emitted before a tick loop is run.

signals.name: before_tick
signals.description: Emitted before a tick is run.

signals.name: after_tick_loop
signals.description: Emitted after the tick loop is run.

signals.name: after_sync
signals.description: Emitted after time is synchronized.  This happens once the NetworkTime is started, and the first time sync process concludes. When running as server, this is emitted instantly after started.

signals.name: after_client_sync
signals.description: Emitted after a client synchronizes their time.  This is only emitted on the server, and is emitted when the client concludes their time sync process. This is useful as this event means that the client is ticking and gameplay has started on their end.

signals.name: on_tickrate_mismatch
signals.description: Emitted when a tickrate mismatch is encountered, and [member NetworkTickrateHandshake.mismatch_action] is set to [constant NetworkTickrateHandshake.SIGNAL].

name: _RollbackSimulationServer
description: Rollback involves restoring the game state to an earlier point in time and re-running game logic from there. This class tracks which nodes participate in rollback, which of them need to be actually simulated, and runs the simulation itself. [br][br]Node simulation honors scene tree order. Processing order and physics processing order are [b]not[/b] considered. [br][br]To participate in rollback, call [method register] using the method to call to simulate the node. Only one [Callable] can be actively registered per node.

methods.name: register
methods.description: Register a [param]callback[/param] to run as part of the rollback loop

methods.name: deregister
methods.description: Deregister a [param]callback[/param] from the rollback loop

methods.name: deregister_node
methods.description: Deregister a [param]node[/param] from the rollback loop

methods.name: register_rollback_input_for
methods.description: Register [param]input[/param] as providing input for [param]node[/param]

methods.name: deregister_rollback_input
methods.description: Deregister [param]input[/param] from providing input for [param]node[/param]

methods.name: is_predicting_current
methods.description: Return true if the currently simulated node is being predicted

methods.name: get_simulated_object
methods.description: Get the currently simulated object

methods.name: simulate
methods.description: Simulate a tick

name: BaseNetInput
briefDescription: Base class for Input nodes used with rollback.

methods.name: _gather
methods.description: Method for gathering input.  This method is supposed to be overridden with your input logic. The input data itself may be gathered outside of this method ( e.g. gathering it over multiple _process calls ), but this is the point where the input variables must be set.  [i]Note:[/i] This is only called for the local player's input nodes.

name: NetworkSchemaSerializer
briefDescription: Base class for serializers, to use with [NetworkSchemas]
description: Each serializer must be able to encode and decode values passed to it. Data is stored in [StreamPeerBuffer] objects. [br][br]To implement a custom serializer, extend this class and pass an instance of it in place of a NetworkSchemaSerializer, for example to [method RollbackSynchronizer.set_schema].

methods.name: encode
methods.description: Encode [param value] into [param buffer]

methods.name: decode
methods.description: Decode a value from [param buffer] and return it

name: RewindableStateMachine
briefDescription: A state machine that can be used with rollback.
description: It relies on [RollbackSynchronizer] to manage its state. State transitions are only triggered by gameplay code, and not by rollback reverting to an earlier state. [br][br]For this node to work correctly, a [RollbackSynchronizer] must be added as a sibling, and it must have the [RewindableStateMachine]'s [member state] property configured as a state property. [br][br]To implement states, extend the [RewindableState] class and add it as a child node.

methods.name: transition
methods.description: Transition to a new state specified by [param new_state_name] and return true.  Finds the given state by name and transitions to it if possible. The new state's [method RewindableState.can_enter] callback decides if it can be entered from the current state. [br][br]Upon transitioning, [method RewindableState.exit] is called on the old state, and [method RewindableState.enter] is called on the new state. In addition, [signal on_state_changed] is emitted. [br][br]Does nothing if transitioning to the currently active state. Emits a warning and does nothing when transitioning to an unknown state.

methods.name: update_states
methods.description: Update the internal cache of known states [br][br]Automatically called on ready and when a child node is added or removed. Call manually to force an update.

members.name: state
members.description: Name of the current state.  Can be an empty string if no state is active. Only modify directly if you need to skip [method transition]'s callbacks.

signals.name: on_state_changed
signals.description: Emitted during state transitions.  This signal can be used to run gameplay code on state changes. [br][br]This signal is emitted whenever a transition happens during rollback, which means it may be emitted multiple times for the same transition if it gets resimulated during rollback. [br][br][b]State changes are not necessarily emitted on all peers.[/b] See: [url=https://foxssake.github.io/netfox/netfox.extras/guides/rewindable-state-machine/#caveats]RewindableStateMachine caveats[/url]

signals.name: on_display_state_changed
signals.description: Emitted after the displayed state has changed.  This signal can be used to update visuals based on state changes. [br][br]This signal is emitted whenever the state after a tick loop has changed.

name: TickInterpolator
briefDescription: Interpolates between network ticks for smooth motion. [br][br]

methods.name: process_settings
methods.description: Process settings. [br][br]Call this after any change to configuration.

methods.name: add_property
methods.description: Add a property to interpolate. [br][br]Settings will be automatically updated. The [param node] may be a string or [NodePath] pointing to a node, or an actual [Node] instance. If the given property is already interpolated, this method does nothing.

methods.name: can_interpolate
methods.description: Check if interpolation can be done. [br][br]Even if it's enabled, no interpolation will be done if there are no properties to interpolate.

methods.name: push_state
methods.description: Record current state for interpolation. [br][br]Note that this will rotate the states, so the previous target becomes the new starting point for the interpolation. This is automatically called if [code]enable_recording[/code] is true.

methods.name: teleport
methods.description: Record current state and transition without interpolation.

members.name: root
members.description: The root node for resolving node paths in properties.

members.name: enabled
members.description: Toggles interpolation.

members.name: properties
members.description: Properties to interpolate.

members.name: record_first_state
members.description: If enabled, takes a snapshot immediately upon instantiation, instead of waiting for the first network tick. Useful for objects that start moving instantly, like projectiles.

members.name: enable_recording
members.description: Toggle automatic state recording. When enabled, the node will take a new snapshot on every network tick. When disabled, call [member push_state] whenever properties are updated.

name: NetworkRigidBody2D
briefDescription: A rollback / state synchronizer class for RigidBody2D. Set state property path to physics_state to synchronize the state of this body.

methods.name: _physics_rollback_tick
methods.description: Override and apply any logic, forces or impulses to the rigid body as you would in physics_process The physics engine will run its simulation during rollback_tick with other nodes

name: NetworkSchemas
description: While some method names are abbreviated, they use a few naming schemes. For example: [br][br][method uint16] - unsigned integer, 16 bits[br][method vec2t] - [Vector2], component of specified [i]type[/i][br][method vec3f32] - [Vector3], each component as a [method float32][br][br]To handle collections, see [method array_of] and [method dictionary].

methods.name: variant
methods.description: Serialize any data type supported by [method @GlobalScope.var_to_bytes]. [br][br]Final size depends on the value.

methods.name: string
methods.description: Serialize strings in UTF-8 encoding. [br][br]Final size depends on the string, the string itself is zero-terminated.

methods.name: bool8
methods.description: Serialize booleans as 8 bits. [br][br]Final size is 1 byte.

methods.name: uint8
methods.description: Serialize unsigned integers as 8 bits. [br][br]Final size is 1 byte.

methods.name: uint16
methods.description: Serialize unsigned integers as 16 bits. [br][br]Final size is 2 bytes.

methods.name: uint32
methods.description: Serialize unsigned integers as 32 bits. [br][br]Final size is 4 bytes.

methods.name: uint64
methods.description: Serialize unsigned integers as 64 bits. [br][br]Final size is 8 bytes.

methods.name: varuint
methods.description: Serialize an unsigned integer as a variable amount of bytes. [br][br]Each byte contains 7 bits of data. The 8th bit indicates whether there are more bytes left. Thus, small numbers fitting into 7 bits will be encoded as a single byte, while larger numbers take more space as they increase. [br][br]Final size is 1 byte for every 7 bits of numeric data.

methods.name: int8
methods.description: Serialize signed integers as 8 bits. [br][br]Final size is 1 byte.

methods.name: int16
methods.description: Serialize signed integers as 16 bits. [br][br]Final size is 2 bytes.

methods.name: int32
methods.description: Serialize signed integers as 32 bits. [br][br]Final size is 4 bytes.

methods.name: int64
methods.description: Serialize signed integers as 64 bits. [br][br]Final size is 8 bytes.

methods.name: float16
methods.description: Serialize floats in half-precision, as 16 bits. [br][br]This is only supported in Godot 4.4 and up, earlier versions fall back to [method float32]. [br][br]Final size is 2 bytes, 4 if using fallback.

methods.name: float32
methods.description: Serialize floats in single-precision, as 32 bits. [br][br]Final size is 4 bytes.

methods.name: float64
methods.description: Serialize floats in double-precision, as 64 bits. [br][br]Final size is 8 bytes.

methods.name: sfrac8
methods.description: Serialize signed fractions in the [code][-1.0, +1.0][/code] range as 8 bits. [br][br]Final size is 1 byte.

methods.name: sfrac16
methods.description: Serialize signed fractions in the [code][-1.0, +1.0][/code] range as 16 bits. [br][br]Final size is 2 bytes.

methods.name: sfrac32
methods.description: Serialize signed fractions in the [code][-1.0, +1.0][/code] range as 32 bits. [br][br]Final size is 4 bytes.

methods.name: ufrac8
methods.description: Serialize signed fractions in the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range as 8 bits. [br][br]Final size is 1 byte.

methods.name: ufrac16
methods.description: Serialize signed fractions in the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range as 16 bits. [br][br]Final size is 2 bytes.

methods.name: ufrac32
methods.description: Serialize signed fractions in the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range as 32 bits. [br][br]Final size is 4 bytes.

methods.name: degrees8
methods.description: Serialize degrees as 8 bits. The value will always decode to the [code][0.0, 360.0)[/code] range. [br][br]Final size is 1 byte.

methods.name: degrees16
methods.description: Serialize degrees as 16 bits. The value will always decode to the [code][0.0, 360.0)[/code] range. [br][br]Final size is 2 bytes.

methods.name: degrees32
methods.description: Serialize degrees as 32 bits. The value will always decode to the [code][0.0, 360.0)[/code] range. [br][br]Final size is 4 bytes.

methods.name: radians8
methods.description: Serialize radians as 8 bits. The value will always decode to the [code][0.0, TAU)[/code] range. [br][br]Final size is 1 byte.

methods.name: radians16
methods.description: Serialize radians as 16 bits. The value will always decode to the [code][0.0, TAU)[/code] range. [br][br]Final size is 2 bytes.

methods.name: radians32
methods.description: Serialize radians as 32 bits. The value will always decode to the [code][0.0, TAU)[/code] range. [br][br]Final size is 4 bytes.

methods.name: vec2t
methods.description: Serialize [Vector2] objects, using [param component_serializer] to serialize each component of the vector. [br][br]Serializes 2 components, size depends on the [param component_serializer].

methods.name: vec2f16
methods.description: Serialize [Vector2] objects, with each component being a half-precision float. [br][br]This is only supported in Godot 4.4 and up. Earlier versions fall back to [method vec2f32]. [br][br]Final size is 4 bytes, 8 if using fallback.

methods.name: vec2f32
methods.description: Serialize [Vector2] objects, with each component being a single-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 8 bytes.

methods.name: vec2f64
methods.description: Serialize [Vector2] objects, with each component being a double-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 16 bytes.

methods.name: vec3t
methods.description: Serialize [Vector3] objects, using [param component_serializer] to serialize each component of the vector. [br][br]Serializes 3 components, size depends on the [param component_serializer].

methods.name: vec3f16
methods.description: Serialize [Vector3] objects, with each component being a half-precision float. [br][br]This is only supported in Godot 4.4 and up. Earlier versions fall back to [method vec3f32]. [br][br]Final size is 6 bytes, 12 if using fallback.

methods.name: vec3f32
methods.description: Serialize [Vector3] objects, with each component being a double-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 12 bytes.

methods.name: vec3f64
methods.description: Serialize [Vector3] objects, with each component being a double-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 24 bytes.

methods.name: vec4t
methods.description: Serialize [Vector4] objects, using [param component_serializer] to serialize each component of the vector. [br][br]Serializes 4 components, size depends on the [param component_serializer].

methods.name: vec4f16
methods.description: Serialize [Vector4] objects, with each component being a half-precision float. [br][br]This is only supported in Godot 4.4 and up. Earlier versions fall back to [method vec4f32]. [br][br]Final size is 8 bytes, 16 if using fallback.

methods.name: vec4f32
methods.description: Serialize [Vector4] objects, with each component being a double-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 16 bytes.

methods.name: vec4f64
methods.description: Serialize [Vector4] objects, with each component being a double-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 32 bytes.

methods.name: normal2t
methods.description: Serialize normalized [Vector2] objects, using [param component_serializer] to serialize each component of the vector. [br][br]Serializes 1 component, size depends on the [param component_serializer].

methods.name: normal2f16
methods.description: Serialize normalized [Vector2] objects, with each component being a half-precision float. [br][br]This is only supported in Godot 4.4 and up. Earlier versions fall back to [method normal2f32]. [br][br]Final size is 2 bytes, 4 if using fallback.

methods.name: normal2f32
methods.description: Serialize normalized [Vector2] objects, with each component being a single-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 4 bytes.

methods.name: normal2f64
methods.description: Serialize normalized [Vector2] objects, with each component being a double-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 8 bytes.

methods.name: normal3t
methods.description: Serialize normalized [Vector3] objects, using [param component_serializer] to serialize each component of the vector. [br][br]Serializes 2 components, size depends on the [param component_serializer].

methods.name: normal3f16
methods.description: Serialize normalized [Vector3] objects, with each component being a half-precision float. [br][br]This is only supported in Godot 4.4 and up. Earlier versions fall back to [method normal3f32]. [br][br]Final size is 4 bytes, 8 if using fallback.

methods.name: normal3f32
methods.description: Serialize normalized [Vector3] objects, with each component being a single-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 8 bytes.

methods.name: normal3f64
methods.description: Serialize normalized [Vector3] objects, with each component being a double-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 16 bytes.

methods.name: quatt
methods.description: Serialize [Quaternion] objects, using [param component_serializer] to serialize each component of the quaternion. [br][br]Serializes 4 components, size depends on the [param component_serializer].

methods.name: quatf16
methods.description: Serialize [Quaternion] objects, with each component being a half-precision float. [br][br]This is only supported in Godot 4.4 and up. Earlier versions fall back to [method quat32f]. [br][br]Final size is 8 bytes, 16 if using fallback.

methods.name: quatf32
methods.description: Serialize [Quaternion] objects, with each component being a single-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 16 bytes.

methods.name: quatf64
methods.description: Serialize [Quaternion] objects, with each component being a double-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 32 bytes.

methods.name: transform2t
methods.description: Serialize [Transform2D] objects, using [param component_serializer] to serialize each component of the transform. [br][br]Serializes a 2x3 matrix in 6 components, final size depends on [param component_serializer].

methods.name: transform2f16
methods.description: Serialize [Transform2D] objects, with each component being a half-precision float. [br][br]This is only supported in Godot 4.4 and up. Earlier versions fall back to [method transform2f32]. [br][br]Final size is 12 bytes, 24 if using fallback.

methods.name: transform2f32
methods.description: Serialize [Transform2D] objects, with each component being a single-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 24 bytes.

methods.name: transform2f64
methods.description: Serialize [Transform2D] objects, with each component being a double-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 48 bytes.

methods.name: transform3t
methods.description: Serialize [Transform3D] objects, using [param component_serializer] to serialize each component of the transform. [br][br]Serializes a 3x4 matrix in 12 components, final size depends on [param component_serializer].

methods.name: transform3f16
methods.description: Serialize [Transform3D] objects, with each component being a half-precision float. [br][br]This is only supported in Godot 4.4 and up. Earlier versions fall back to [method transform3f32]. [br][br]Final size is 24 bytes, 48 if using fallback.

methods.name: transform3f32
methods.description: Serialize [Transform3D] objects, with each component being a single-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 48 bytes.

methods.name: transform3f64
methods.description: Serialize [Transform2D] objects, with each component being a double-precision float. [br][br]Final size is 96 bytes.

methods.name: array_of
methods.description: Serialize homogenoeous arrays, using [param item_serializer] to serialize each item, and [param size_serializer] to serialize the array's size. [br][br]To serialize heterogenoeous arrays, use [method variant] as the item serializer. [br][br]Final size is [code]sizeof(size_serializer) + array.size() * sizeof(item_serializer)[/code]

methods.name: dictionary
methods.description: Serialize homogenoeous dictionaries, using [param key_serialize] and [param value_serializer] to serialize key-value pairs, and [param size_serializer] to serialize the number of entries. [br][br]If either the keys or values are not homogenoeous, use [method variant]. [br][br]Final size is [code]sizeof(size_serializer) + dictionary.size() * (sizeof(key_serializer) + sizeof(value_serializer))[/code]

name: _NetworkHistoryServer
briefDescription: Tracks the history of objects' properties
description: Specifically, history is stored for rollback state properties, rollback input properties, and synchronized state properties. [br][br]Keeping history lets rollback restore earlier game states for resimulation, and enables [_NetworkSynchronizationServer] to send diff states by comparing against historical data.

methods.name: register_rollback_state
methods.description: Register a rollback state property

methods.name: deregister_rollback_state
methods.description: Deregister a rollback state property

methods.name: register_rollback_input
methods.description: Register a rollback input property

methods.name: deregister_rollback_input
methods.description: Deregister a rollback input property

methods.name: register_sync_state
methods.description: Register a synchronized state property

methods.name: deregister_sync_state
methods.description: Deregister a synchronized state property

methods.name: deregister
methods.description: Deregister a node, no longer tracking any property it had registered using any of the [code]register_*()[/code] methods

methods.name: get_latest_state_tick_for
methods.description: Return the latest tick where any of the [param]subjects[/param] had rollback state data available

methods.name: get_state_age_for
methods.description: Return how old is the latest rollback state data for any of the [param] subjects[/param], in ticks

methods.name: get_latest_input_for
methods.description: Return the latest tick where any of the [param]subjects[/param] had rollback input data available

methods.name: get_input_age_for
methods.description: Return how old is the latest rollback input data for any of the [param] subjects[/param], in ticks

name: NorayProtocolHandler
briefDescription: This class parses incoming data from noray's protocol.
description: Unless you're writing your own noray integration, [Noray] should cover most use cases.

methods.name: reset
methods.description: Resets the parser.

methods.name: ingest
methods.description: Parse an incoming piece of data.

signals.name: on_command
signals.description: Emitted for every command parsed during a [method ingest] call.

methods.name: _init_physics_space
methods.description: Override this method to initialize the physics space.

methods.name: _physics_step
methods.description: Override this method to take one step in the physics space. [br][br]It should also flush and update all Godot nodes

methods.name: _snapshot_space
methods.description: Override this method to record the current state of the physics space.

methods.name: _rollback_space
methods.description: Override this method to restore the physics space to a previous state.

name: PredictiveSynchronizer
briefDescription: Similar to [RollbackSynchronizer], this class manages local variables in a rollback context for predictive simulation without networking.
description: This is a simplified version that focuses on local state management. [br][br]Like [RollbackSynchronizer], it automatically discovers nodes with a [code]_rollback_tick(delta: float, tick: int)[/code] method and calls them during the prediction phase.

methods.name: process_settings
methods.description: Process settings. [br][br]Call this after any change to configuration.

methods.name: spawn
methods.description: Mark the spawn tick for all nodes managed by this synchronizer. [br][br]When rewinding to a tick earlier than the spawn tick, every managed node will be deactivated.

methods.name: despawn
methods.description: Mark the despawn tick for all nodes managed by this synchronizer. [br][br]When rewinding to a tick later than the despawn tick, every managed node will be deactivated.

methods.name: is_alive
methods.description: Return true if nodes managed by this synchronizer are alive. [br][br]Note that this method assumes that all node liveness is managed by the synchronizer. If some node livenesses are handled separately, this method may return the wrong liveness. In that case, use [method _RollbackLivenessServer.is_alive] and check for individual nodes.

methods.name: add_state
methods.description: Add a state property. [br][br]Settings will be automatically updated. The [param node] may be a string or [NodePath] pointing to a node, or an actual [Node] instance. If the given property is already tracked, this method does nothing.

members.name: root
members.description: The root node for resolving node paths in properties. Defaults to the parent node.

members.name: state_properties
members.description: Properties that define the game state. [br][br]State properties are recorded for each tick and restored during rollback. State is restored before every rollback tick, and recorded after simulating the tick.

name: RewindableAction
briefDescription: Represents actions that may or may not happen, in a way compatible with rollback.

methods.name: status_string
methods.description: Returns the [param status] enum as string

methods.name: set_active
methods.description: Toggles the action for a given [param tick]

methods.name: is_active
methods.description: Check if the action is happening for the given [param tick]

methods.name: get_status
methods.description: Check the action's status for the given [param tick] [br][br]Returns [constant ACTIVE] if the action is happening.[br]Returns [constant INACTIVE] if the action is not happening.[br]Returns [constant CONFIRMING] if the action was previously known as not happening, but now it is.[br]Returns [constant CANCELLING] if the action was previously known to be happening, but now it is not.[br][br]The [constant CONFIRMING] and [constant CANCELLING] statuses may occur if the action was just toggled, or data was received from the action's authority.

methods.name: has_confirmed
methods.description: Returns true if the action has been in [constant CONFIRMING] status during the last tick loop

methods.name: has_cancelled
methods.description: Returns true if the action has been in [constant CANCELLING] status during the last tick loop

methods.name: get_status_string
methods.description: Get the action's current status as a string [br][br]See also: [member get_status]

methods.name: has_context
methods.description: Returns true if the action has any stored context for the given [param tick]

methods.name: get_context
methods.description: Get the context stored for the given [param tick], or null

methods.name: set_context
methods.description: Store [param value] as the context for the given [param tick]

methods.name: erase_context
methods.description: Erase the context for the given [param tick]

methods.name: mutate
methods.description: Whenever the action happens, mutate the [param target] object [br][br]See also: [method _NetworkRollback.mutate]

methods.name: dont_mutate
methods.description: Remove the [param target] object from the list of objects to [method mutate] [br][br]See also: [method _NetworkRollback.mutate]

name: RewindableRandomNumberGenerator
briefDescription: Provides methods for generating pseudo-random numbers in the rollback tick loop.
description: Using a regular [RandomNumberGenerator] in [code]_rollback_tick()[/code] would generate different numbers on each peer. It also generates different numbers when resimulating the same tick. [br][br]This class solves all of the above, making it suitable for use during rollback. [br][br]The seed must be provided on instantiation, and must be the same on all peers for the random number generator to work properly.

methods.name: randf
methods.description: Returns a pseudo-random float between [code]0.0[/code] and [code]1.0[/code] (inclusive).

methods.name: randf_range
methods.description: Returns a pseudo-random float between [code]from[/code] and [code]to[/code] (inclusive).

methods.name: randfn
methods.description: Returns a normally-distributed, pseudo-random floating-point number from the specified [code]mean[/code] and a standard [code]deviation[/code]. This is also known as a Gaussian distribution.

methods.name: randi
methods.description: Returns a pseudo-random 32-bit unsigned integer between [code]0[/code] and [code]4294967295[/code] (inclusive).

methods.name: randi_range
methods.description: Returns a pseudo-random 32-bit unsigned integer between [code]from[/code] and [code]to[/code] (inclusive).
